Gym owners, welcome to the video about sales appointment availability. So this video was one that we made after the fact, after realizing that what we thought was obvious was not. So I'm going to explain why sales appointment availability will massively increase the ROI of your campaign or destroy your campaign based on fundamental principles of customers, which is making it convenient for them to come in. So availability comes down to three things. First is the amount of time that you, time slots that you have in the day at what time of day you have them. The second piece is how many days per week you have those time slots. And the third piece is how many people that you permit per time slot. So what I'm going to tell you is I'll tell you two stories and I'll tell you the right way to do it. There was a gym owner who was struggling and was like, I can't get leads to show up, why isn't it, why is anyone showing like this doesn't work, blah, blah, blah. And I was like, all right, let me make sure your links work. Like your numbers are crazy low. Like this doesn't make sense. So I clicked his link and then I saw that his availability was 7.15 in the morning. That's it. And so he broke almost every rule that I'm about to show you. And he was doing group sales only on a couple of days a week at 7.15 in the morning. No shit no one could make it. Obviously no one can make that time. So like you have to remember that these are human beings. Like imagine a grocery store that's only open from 7 to 15 to 8am. How well do you think they're going to do? Probably not that well because convenience matters. That's why businesses are open 24 hours a day because they know they can make more sales over that period of time. So that's story number one. Story number two is these variables can matter a lot even with small changes. We went from 6 days per week to 5 days per week and saw a 30% decrease in the number of appointments that were scheduled. We went from 67% scheduling appointments to 49% scheduling appointments. That's bad. That's a massive decrease. It's not just an 18% decrease. That's the absolute number. So going from 67 to 49 is almost decreasing by a third. It's enormous. I just want to make sure it's a little less than 25%. The point is it's a huge decrease. Imagine if you just get 25% less sales every month simply because you were open 5 days a week instead of 6 for sales appointments. That's massive. So don't do that. I'll show you the best thing in a second but I just want to explain the story. The second thing is that we actually went from 3pm to 8pm. That is what we had as our standard availability for people walking in the door. What we did was we actually changed this to 12 to 8pm. So noon to 8. By doing that, we were able to push our show rates from 49% to 59%. Now a 10% increase of 49% would be 54%. This is a 20% increase, actually a little like 22% increase, from 49% to 59%. So just as much as this decreased, this increased the amount of people who were walking in the door showing up. This was a schedule rate thing. This was a show rate thing. So what I want to show you is what I consider the bare minimum of what you should have as your sales appointment availability if you want to make money and you want to scale your gym. One of my biggest pet peeves are people who say they are busy and broke. You can't be both busy and broke because you can always trade time for money. So you can always trade time for money. If you have time, you can trade it for money. If you have money, you can trade it for time. They go back and forth. So this is the best setup that we have found. Sorry, the minimum setup that you should have. Three to eight PM, if you want to be better and make more sales, make it from 12 to eight. If you want to make the most amount of sales, be minimum open six days a week. The third piece is availability per time slot. This guy. I'd recommend three per time slot because you're only going to have 50 to 70% of show. It depends on the market. But that means that if you have 50% that show, when you have three, you're always going to have one that shows. And then very likely you'll have something like every other time you'll have two that show. If you get uncomfortable with that and you'd rather just have zero or one, then you can decrease this to two. So just like that's up to you. I think that two on ones and one on one sales are about the same thing. But if you're like, I'm just starting out, you can start that at two and that's okay. All right. Now one of the biggest no-nos that I will say to everyone is no group. Don't sell in groups. Why? Because it's not efficient. I guarantee for a number of reasons. One, if you're selling in groups, it's because you're trying to limit your availability. So automatically your ROI and your campaigns is going to go down. Furthermore, you're going to get a lower average ticket because you're not going to be able to personalize and really get the whales when they're in the room. Third, you're actually going to close at a lower percentage again because you can't personalize. And so you stack all that stuff on top. It's really, really, really ridiculously hard to even match one-on-one. And more realistically, you're going to be at half or a third of the ROI of the campaign by getting lazy. And so if you're telling me I'm trying to make more, then make more. Don't be lazy. Okay? Now, if you're like, okay, I can't take any more appointments. I'm so stacked. A, that's a good problem. But you don't want to change the process. You just change the person doing the process so you can find someone else who sells for you in a one-on-one environment. Or you can both sell one-on-one. If you go into an LA fitness, you go into a 24-hour fitness, are they doing group sales? Of course not. They've got eight guys doing one-on-one sales. Why? Because they've done this for 50 years and they already know that this is the best way to sell because they know that they can maximally extract value and match the customer with the program and the service that works best for them. Okay? If you want to help people and you want to make more money, do one-on-ones. Hope that makes sense. So, that should conclude the sales availability. I hope I hammered this home. If you want to make money, you want to get people in the door, you want to help more people, make sure that it's convenient for them so they can actually come in when it works so that you can help them. All right? See you in the next video.
